2400 *		current search path.  Visible means "would be found by searching
2401 *		for the unqualified collation name".
2402 *
2403 * Note that only collations that work with the current database's encoding
2404 * will be considered visible.
2405 */
2406bool
2407CollationIsVisible(Oid collid)
2408{
2409	return CollationIsVisibleExt(collid, NULL);
2410}
2411
2412/*
2413 * CollationIsVisibleExt
2414 *		As above, but if the collation isn't found and is_missing is not NULL,
2415 *		then set *is_missing = true and return false instead of throwing
2416 *		an error.  (Caller must initialize *is_missing = false.)
2417 */
2418static bool
2419CollationIsVisibleExt(Oid collid, bool *is_missing)
2420{
2421	HeapTuple	colltup;
2422	Form_pg_collation collform;
2423	Oid			collnamespace;
2424	bool		visible;
2425
2426	colltup = SearchSysCache1(COLLOID, ObjectIdGetDatum(collid));
2427	if (!HeapTupleIsValid(colltup))
2428	{
2429		if (is_missing != NULL)
2430		{
2431			*is_missing = true;
2432			return false;
2433		}
2434		elog(ERROR, "cache lookup failed for collation %u", collid);
2435	}
2436	collform = (Form_pg_collation) GETSTRUCT(colltup);
2437
2438	recomputeNamespacePath();
2439
2440	/*
2441	 * Quick check: if it ain't in the path at all, it ain't visible. Items in
2442	 * the system namespace are surely in the path and so we needn't even do
2443	 * list_member_oid() for them.
2444	 */
2445	collnamespace = collform->collnamespace;
2446	if (collnamespace != PG_CATALOG_NAMESPACE &&
2447		!list_member_oid(activeSearchPath, collnamespace))
2448		visible = false;
2449	else
2450	{
2451		/*
2452		 * If it is in the path, it might still not be visible; it could be
2453		 * hidden by another collation of the same name earlier in the path,
2454		 * or it might not work with the current DB encoding.  So we must do a
2455		 * slow check to see if this collation would be found by
2456		 * CollationGetCollid.
2457		 */
2458		char	   *collname = NameStr(collform->collname);
2459
2460		visible = (CollationGetCollid(collname) == collid);
2461	}
2462
2463	ReleaseSysCache(colltup);
2464
2465	return visible;
2466}
2467
2468
2469/*
2470 * ConversionGetConid
2471 *		Try to resolve an unqualified conversion name.
2472 *		Returns OID if conversion found in search path, else InvalidOid.
2473 *
2474 * This is essentially the same as RelnameGetRelid.
2475 */
2476Oid
2477ConversionGetConid(const char *conname)
2478{
2479	Oid			conid;
2480	ListCell   *l;
2481
2482	recomputeNamespacePath();
2483
2484	foreach(l, activeSearchPath)
2485	{
2486		Oid			namespaceId = lfirst_oid(l);
2487
2488		if (namespaceId == myTempNamespace)
2489			continue;			/* do not look in temp namespace */
2490
2491		conid = GetSysCacheOid2(CONNAMENSP, Anum_pg_conversion_oid,
2492								PointerGetDatum(conname),
2493								ObjectIdGetDatum(namespaceId));
2494		if (OidIsValid(conid))
2495			return conid;
2496	}
2497
2498	/* Not found in path */
2499	return InvalidOid;
2500}
2501
2502/*
2503 * ConversionIsVisible
2504 *		Determine whether a conversion (identified by OID) is visible in the
2505 *		current search path.  Visible means "would be found by searching
2506 *		for the unqualified conversion name".
2507 */
2508bool
2509ConversionIsVisible(Oid conid)
2510{
2511	return ConversionIsVisibleExt(conid, NULL);
2512}
2513
2514/*
2515 * ConversionIsVisibleExt
2516 *		As above, but if the conversion isn't found and is_missing is not NULL,
2517 *		then set *is_missing = true and return false instead of throwing
2518 *		an error.  (Caller must initialize *is_missing = false.)
2519 */
2520static bool
2521ConversionIsVisibleExt(Oid conid, bool *is_missing)
2522{
2523	HeapTuple	contup;
2524	Form_pg_conversion conform;
2525	Oid			connamespace;
2526	bool		visible;
2527
2528	contup = SearchSysCache1(CONVOID, ObjectIdGetDatum(conid));
2529	if (!HeapTupleIsValid(contup))
2530	{
2531		if (is_missing != NULL)
2532		{
2533			*is_missing = true;
2534			return false;
2535		}
2536		elog(ERROR, "cache lookup failed for conversion %u", conid);
2537	}
2538	conform = (Form_pg_conversion) GETSTRUCT(contup);
2539
2540	recomputeNamespacePath();
2541
2542	/*
2543	 * Quick check: if it ain't in the path at all, it ain't visible. Items in
2544	 * the system namespace are surely in the path and so we needn't even do
2545	 * list_member_oid() for them.
2546	 */
2547	connamespace = conform->connamespace;
2548	if (connamespace != PG_CATALOG_NAMESPACE &&
2549		!list_member_oid(activeSearchPath, connamespace))
2550		visible = false;
2551	else
2552	{
2553		/*
2554		 * If it is in the path, it might still not be visible; it could be
2555		 * hidden by another conversion of the same name earlier in the path.
2556		 * So we must do a slow check to see if this conversion would be found
2557		 * by ConversionGetConid.
2558		 */
2559		char	   *conname = NameStr(conform->conname);
2560
2561		visible = (ConversionGetConid(conname) == conid);
2562	}
2563
2564	ReleaseSysCache(contup);
2565
2566	return visible;
2567}
2568
2569/*
2570 * get_statistics_object_oid - find a statistics object by possibly qualified name
2571 *
2572 * If not found, returns InvalidOid if missing_ok, else throws error
2573 */
2574Oid
2575get_statistics_object_oid(List *names, bool missing_ok)
2576{
2577	char	   *schemaname;
2578	char	   *stats_name;
2579	Oid			namespaceId;
2580	Oid			stats_oid = InvalidOid;
2581	ListCell   *l;
2582
2583	/* deconstruct the name list */
2584	DeconstructQualifiedName(names, &schemaname, &stats_name);
2585
2586	if (schemaname)
2587	{
2588		/* use exact schema given */
2589		namespaceId = LookupExplicitNamespace(schemaname, missing_ok);
2590		if (missing_ok && !OidIsValid(namespaceId))
2591			stats_oid = InvalidOid;
2592		else
2593			stats_oid = GetSysCacheOid2(STATEXTNAMENSP, Anum_pg_statistic_ext_oid,
2594										PointerGetDatum(stats_name),
2595										ObjectIdGetDatum(namespaceId));
2596	}
2597	else
2598	{
2599		/* search for it in search path */
2600		recomputeNamespacePath();
2601
2602		foreach(l, activeSearchPath)
2603		{
2604			namespaceId = lfirst_oid(l);
2605
2606			if (namespaceId == myTempNamespace)
2607				continue;		/* do not look in temp namespace */
2608			stats_oid = GetSysCacheOid2(STATEXTNAMENSP, Anum_pg_statistic_ext_oid,
2609										PointerGetDatum(stats_name),
2610										ObjectIdGetDatum(namespaceId));
2611			if (OidIsValid(stats_oid))
2612				break;
2613		}
2614	}
2615
2616	if (!OidIsValid(stats_oid) && !missing_ok)
2617		ereport(ERROR,
2618				(errcode(ERRCODE_UNDEFINED_OBJECT),
2619				 errmsg("statistics object \"%s\" does not exist",
2620						NameListToString(names))));
2621
2622	return stats_oid;
2623}
2624
2625/*
2626 * StatisticsObjIsVisible
2627 *		Determine whether a statistics object (identified by OID) is visible in
2628 *		the current search path.  Visible means "would be found by searching
2629 *		for the unqualified statistics object name".
2630 */
2631bool
2632StatisticsObjIsVisible(Oid stxid)
2633{
2634	return StatisticsObjIsVisibleExt(stxid, NULL);
2635}
2636
2637/*
2638 * StatisticsObjIsVisibleExt
2639 *		As above, but if the statistics object isn't found and is_missing is
2640 *		not NULL, then set *is_missing = true and return false instead of
2641 *		throwing an error.  (Caller must initialize *is_missing = false.)
2642 */
2643static bool
2644StatisticsObjIsVisibleExt(Oid stxid, bool *is_missing)
2645{
2646	HeapTuple	stxtup;
2647	Form_pg_statistic_ext stxform;
2648	Oid			stxnamespace;
2649	bool		visible;
2650
2651	stxtup = SearchSysCache1(STATEXTOID, ObjectIdGetDatum(stxid));
2652	if (!HeapTupleIsValid(stxtup))
2653	{
2654		if (is_missing != NULL)
2655		{
2656			*is_missing = true;
2657			return false;
2658		}
2659		elog(ERROR, "cache lookup failed for statistics object %u", stxid);
2660	}
2661	stxform = (Form_pg_statistic_ext) GETSTRUCT(stxtup);
2662
2663	recomputeNamespacePath();
2664
2665	/*
2666	 * Quick check: if it ain't in the path at all, it ain't visible. Items in
2667	 * the system namespace are surely in the path and so we needn't even do
2668	 * list_member_oid() for them.
2669	 */
2670	stxnamespace = stxform->stxnamespace;
2671	if (stxnamespace != PG_CATALOG_NAMESPACE &&
2672		!list_member_oid(activeSearchPath, stxnamespace))
2673		visible = false;
2674	else
2675	{
2676		/*
2677		 * If it is in the path, it might still not be visible; it could be
2678		 * hidden by another statistics object of the same name earlier in the
2679		 * path. So we must do a slow check for conflicting objects.
2680		 */
2681		char	   *stxname = NameStr(stxform->stxname);
2682		ListCell   *l;
2683
2684		visible = false;
2685		foreach(l, activeSearchPath)
2686		{
2687			Oid			namespaceId = lfirst_oid(l);
2688
2689			if (namespaceId == myTempNamespace)
2690				continue;		/* do not look in temp namespace */
2691
2692			if (namespaceId == stxnamespace)
2693			{
2694				/* Found it first in path */
2695				visible = true;
2696				break;
2697			}
2698			if (SearchSysCacheExists2(STATEXTNAMENSP,
2699									  PointerGetDatum(stxname),
2700									  ObjectIdGetDatum(namespaceId)))
2701			{
2702				/* Found something else first in path */
2703				break;
2704			}
2705		}
2706	}
2707
2708	ReleaseSysCache(stxtup);
2709
2710	return visible;
2711}
2712
2713/*
2714 * get_ts_parser_oid - find a TS parser by possibly qualified name
2715 *
2716 * If not found, returns InvalidOid if missing_ok, else throws error
2717 */
2718Oid
2719get_ts_parser_oid(List *names, bool missing_ok)
2720{
2721	char	   *schemaname;
2722	char	   *parser_name;
2723	Oid			namespaceId;
2724	Oid			prsoid = InvalidOid;
2725	ListCell   *l;
2726
2727	/* deconstruct the name list */
2728	DeconstructQualifiedName(names, &schemaname, &parser_name);
2729
2730	if (schemaname)
2731	{
2732		/* use exact schema given */
2733		namespaceId = LookupExplicitNamespace(schemaname, missing_ok);
2734		if (missing_ok && !OidIsValid(namespaceId))
2735			prsoid = InvalidOid;
2736		else
2737			prsoid = GetSysCacheOid2(TSPARSERNAMENSP, Anum_pg_ts_parser_oid,
2738									 PointerGetDatum(parser_name),
2739									 ObjectIdGetDatum(namespaceId));
2740	}
2741	else
2742	{
2743		/* search for it in search path */
2744		recomputeNamespacePath();
2745
2746		foreach(l, activeSearchPath)
2747		{
2748			namespaceId = lfirst_oid(l);
2749
2750			if (namespaceId == myTempNamespace)
2751				continue;		/* do not look in temp namespace */
2752
2753			prsoid = GetSysCacheOid2(TSPARSERNAMENSP, Anum_pg_ts_parser_oid,
2754									 PointerGetDatum(parser_name),
2755									 ObjectIdGetDatum(namespaceId));
2756			if (OidIsValid(prsoid))
2757				break;
2758		}
2759	}
2760
2761	if (!OidIsValid(prsoid) && !missing_ok)
2762		ereport(ERROR,
2763				(errcode(ERRCODE_UNDEFINED_OBJECT),
2764				 errmsg("text search parser \"%s\" does not exist",
2765						NameListToString(names))));
2766
2767	return prsoid;
2768}
2769
2770/*
2771 * TSParserIsVisible
2772 *		Determine whether a parser (identified by OID) is visible in the
2773 *		current search path.  Visible means "would be found by searching
2774 *		for the unqualified parser name".
2775 */
2776bool
2777TSParserIsVisible(Oid prsId)
2778{
2779	return TSParserIsVisibleExt(prsId, NULL);
2780}
2781
2782/*
2783 * TSParserIsVisibleExt
2784 *		As above, but if the parser isn't found and is_missing is not NULL,
2785 *		then set *is_missing = true and return false instead of throwing
2786 *		an error.  (Caller must initialize *is_missing = false.)
2787 */
2788static bool
2789TSParserIsVisibleExt(Oid prsId, bool *is_missing)
2790{
2791	HeapTuple	tup;
2792	Form_pg_ts_parser form;
2793	Oid			namespace;
2794	bool		visible;
2795
2796	tup = SearchSysCache1(TSPARSEROID, ObjectIdGetDatum(prsId));
2797	if (!HeapTupleIsValid(tup))
2798	{
2799		if (is_missing != NULL)
2800		{
2801			*is_missing = true;
2802			return false;
2803		}
2804		elog(ERROR, "cache lookup failed for text search parser %u", prsId);
2805	}
2806	form = (Form_pg_ts_parser) GETSTRUCT(tup);
2807
2808	recomputeNamespacePath();
2809
2810	/*
2811	 * Quick check: if it ain't in the path at all, it ain't visible. Items in
2812	 * the system namespace are surely in the path and so we needn't even do
2813	 * list_member_oid() for them.
2814	 */
2815	namespace = form->prsnamespace;
2816	if (namespace != PG_CATALOG_NAMESPACE &&
2817		!list_member_oid(activeSearchPath, namespace))
2818		visible = false;
2819	else
2820	{
2821		/*
2822		 * If it is in the path, it might still not be visible; it could be
2823		 * hidden by another parser of the same name earlier in the path. So
2824		 * we must do a slow check for conflicting parsers.
2825		 */
2826		char	   *name = NameStr(form->prsname);
2827		ListCell   *l;
2828
2829		visible = false;
2830		foreach(l, activeSearchPath)
2831		{
2832			Oid			namespaceId = lfirst_oid(l);
2833
2834			if (namespaceId == myTempNamespace)
2835				continue;		/* do not look in temp namespace */
2836
2837			if (namespaceId == namespace)
2838			{
2839				/* Found it first in path */
2840				visible = true;
2841				break;
2842			}
2843			if (SearchSysCacheExists2(TSPARSERNAMENSP,
2844									  PointerGetDatum(name),
2845									  ObjectIdGetDatum(namespaceId)))
2846			{
2847				/* Found something else first in path */
2848				break;
2849			}
2850		}
2851	}
2852
2853	ReleaseSysCache(tup);
2854
2855	return visible;
2856}
2857
2858/*
2859 * get_ts_dict_oid - find a TS dictionary by possibly qualified name
2860 *
2861 * If not found, returns InvalidOid if missing_ok, else throws error
2862 */
2863Oid
2864get_ts_dict_oid(List *names, bool missing_ok)
2865{
2866	char	   *schemaname;
2867	char	   *dict_name;
2868	Oid			namespaceId;
2869	Oid			dictoid = InvalidOid;
2870	ListCell   *l;
2871
2872	/* deconstruct the name list */
2873	DeconstructQualifiedName(names, &schemaname, &dict_name);
2874
2875	if (schemaname)
2876	{
2877		/* use exact schema given */
2878		namespaceId = LookupExplicitNamespace(schemaname, missing_ok);
2879		if (missing_ok && !OidIsValid(namespaceId))
2880			dictoid = InvalidOid;
2881		else
2882			dictoid = GetSysCacheOid2(TSDICTNAMENSP, Anum_pg_ts_dict_oid,
2883									  PointerGetDatum(dict_name),
2884									  ObjectIdGetDatum(namespaceId));
2885	}
2886	else
2887	{
2888		/* search for it in search path */
2889		recomputeNamespacePath();
2890
2891		foreach(l, activeSearchPath)
2892		{
2893			namespaceId = lfirst_oid(l);
2894
2895			if (namespaceId == myTempNamespace)
2896				continue;		/* do not look in temp namespace */
2897
2898			dictoid = GetSysCacheOid2(TSDICTNAMENSP, Anum_pg_ts_dict_oid,
2899									  PointerGetDatum(dict_name),
2900									  ObjectIdGetDatum(namespaceId));
2901			if (OidIsValid(dictoid))
2902				break;
2903		}
2904	}
2905
2906	if (!OidIsValid(dictoid) && !missing_ok)
2907		ereport(ERROR,
2908				(errcode(ERRCODE_UNDEFINED_OBJECT),
2909				 errmsg("text search dictionary \"%s\" does not exist",
2910						NameListToString(names))));
2911
2912	return dictoid;
2913}
2914
2915/*
2916 * TSDictionaryIsVisible
2917 *		Determine whether a dictionary (identified by OID) is visible in the
2918 *		current search path.  Visible means "would be found by searching
2919 *		for the unqualified dictionary name".
2920 */
2921bool
2922TSDictionaryIsVisible(Oid dictId)
2923{
2924	return TSDictionaryIsVisibleExt(dictId, NULL);
2925}
2926
2927/*
2928 * TSDictionaryIsVisibleExt
2929 *		As above, but if the dictionary isn't found and is_missing is not NULL,
2930 *		then set *is_missing = true and return false instead of throwing
2931 *		an error.  (Caller must initialize *is_missing = false.)
2932 */
2933static bool
2934TSDictionaryIsVisibleExt(Oid dictId, bool *is_missing)
2935{
2936	HeapTuple	tup;
2937	Form_pg_ts_dict form;
2938	Oid			namespace;
2939	bool		visible;
2940
2941	tup = SearchSysCache1(TSDICTOID, ObjectIdGetDatum(dictId));
2942	if (!HeapTupleIsValid(tup))
2943	{
2944		if (is_missing != NULL)
2945		{
2946			*is_missing = true;
2947			return false;
2948		}
2949		elog(ERROR, "cache lookup failed for text search dictionary %u",
2950			 dictId);
2951	}
2952	form = (Form_pg_ts_dict) GETSTRUCT(tup);
2953
2954	recomputeNamespacePath();
2955
2956	/*
2957	 * Quick check: if it ain't in the path at all, it ain't visible. Items in
2958	 * the system namespace are surely in the path and so we needn't even do
2959	 * list_member_oid() for them.
2960	 */
2961	namespace = form->dictnamespace;
2962	if (namespace != PG_CATALOG_NAMESPACE &&
2963		!list_member_oid(activeSearchPath, namespace))
2964		visible = false;
2965	else
2966	{
2967		/*
2968		 * If it is in the path, it might still not be visible; it could be
2969		 * hidden by another dictionary of the same name earlier in the path.
2970		 * So we must do a slow check for conflicting dictionaries.
2971		 */
2972		char	   *name = NameStr(form->dictname);
2973		ListCell   *l;
2974
2975		visible = false;
2976		foreach(l, activeSearchPath)
2977		{
2978			Oid			namespaceId = lfirst_oid(l);
2979
2980			if (namespaceId == myTempNamespace)
2981				continue;		/* do not look in temp namespace */
2982
2983			if (namespaceId == namespace)
2984			{
2985				/* Found it first in path */
2986				visible = true;
2987				break;
2988			}
2989			if (SearchSysCacheExists2(TSDICTNAMENSP,
2990									  PointerGetDatum(name),
2991									  ObjectIdGetDatum(namespaceId)))
2992			{
2993				/* Found something else first in path */
2994				break;
2995			}
2996		}
2997	}
2998
2999	ReleaseSysCache(tup);
3000
3001	return visible;
3002}
3003
3004/*
3005 * get_ts_template_oid - find a TS template by possibly qualified name
3006 *
3007 * If not found, returns InvalidOid if missing_ok, else throws error
3008 */
3009Oid
3010get_ts_template_oid(List *names, bool missing_ok)
3011{
3012	char	   *schemaname;
3013	char	   *template_name;
3014	Oid			namespaceId;
3015	Oid			tmploid = InvalidOid;
3016	ListCell   *l;
3017
3018	/* deconstruct the name list */
3019	DeconstructQualifiedName(names, &schemaname, &template_name);
3020
3021	if (schemaname)
3022	{
3023		/* use exact schema given */
3024		namespaceId = LookupExplicitNamespace(schemaname, missing_ok);
3025		if (missing_ok && !OidIsValid(namespaceId))
3026			tmploid = InvalidOid;
3027		else
3028			tmploid = GetSysCacheOid2(TSTEMPLATENAMENSP, Anum_pg_ts_template_oid,
3029									  PointerGetDatum(template_name),
3030									  ObjectIdGetDatum(namespaceId));
3031	}
3032	else
3033	{
3034		/* search for it in search path */
3035		recomputeNamespacePath();
3036
3037		foreach(l, activeSearchPath)
3038		{
3039			namespaceId = lfirst_oid(l);
3040
3041			if (namespaceId == myTempNamespace)
3042				continue;		/* do not look in temp namespace */
3043
3044			tmploid = GetSysCacheOid2(TSTEMPLATENAMENSP, Anum_pg_ts_template_oid,
3045									  PointerGetDatum(template_name),
3046									  ObjectIdGetDatum(namespaceId));
3047			if (OidIsValid(tmploid))
3048				break;
3049		}
3050	}
3051
3052	if (!OidIsValid(tmploid) && !missing_ok)
3053		ereport(ERROR,
3054				(errcode(ERRCODE_UNDEFINED_OBJECT),
3055				 errmsg("text search template \"%s\" does not exist",
3056						NameListToString(names))));
3057
3058	return tmploid;
3059}
3060
3061/*
3062 * TSTemplateIsVisible
3063 *		Determine whether a template (identified by OID) is visible in the
3064 *		current search path.  Visible means "would be found by searching
3065 *		for the unqualified template name".
3066 */
3067bool
3068TSTemplateIsVisible(Oid tmplId)
3069{
3070	return TSTemplateIsVisibleExt(tmplId, NULL);
3071}
3072
3073/*
3074 * TSTemplateIsVisibleExt
3075 *		As above, but if the template isn't found and is_missing is not NULL,
3076 *		then set *is_missing = true and return false instead of throwing
3077 *		an error.  (Caller must initialize *is_missing = false.)
3078 */
3079static bool
3080TSTemplateIsVisibleExt(Oid tmplId, bool *is_missing)
3081{
3082	HeapTuple	tup;
3083	Form_pg_ts_template form;
3084	Oid			namespace;
3085	bool		visible;
3086
3087	tup = SearchSysCache1(TSTEMPLATEOID, ObjectIdGetDatum(tmplId));
3088	if (!HeapTupleIsValid(tup))
3089	{
3090		if (is_missing != NULL)
3091		{
3092			*is_missing = true;
3093			return false;
3094		}
3095		elog(ERROR, "cache lookup failed for text search template %u", tmplId);
3096	}
3097	form = (Form_pg_ts_template) GETSTRUCT(tup);
3098
3099	recomputeNamespacePath();
3100
3101	/*
3102	 * Quick check: if it ain't in the path at all, it ain't visible. Items in
3103	 * the system namespace are surely in the path and so we needn't even do
3104	 * list_member_oid() for them.
3105	 */
3106	namespace = form->tmplnamespace;
3107	if (namespace != PG_CATALOG_NAMESPACE &&
3108		!list_member_oid(activeSearchPath, namespace))
3109		visible = false;
3110	else
3111	{
3112		/*
3113		 * If it is in the path, it might still not be visible; it could be
3114		 * hidden by another template of the same name earlier in the path. So
3115		 * we must do a slow check for conflicting templates.
3116		 */
3117		char	   *name = NameStr(form->tmplname);
3118		ListCell   *l;
3119
3120		visible = false;
3121		foreach(l, activeSearchPath)
3122		{
3123			Oid			namespaceId = lfirst_oid(l);
3124
3125			if (namespaceId == myTempNamespace)
3126				continue;		/* do not look in temp namespace */
3127
3128			if (namespaceId == namespace)
3129			{
3130				/* Found it first in path */
3131				visible = true;
3132				break;
3133			}
3134			if (SearchSysCacheExists2(TSTEMPLATENAMENSP,
3135									  PointerGetDatum(name),
3136									  ObjectIdGetDatum(namespaceId)))
3137			{
3138				/* Found something else first in path */
3139				break;
3140			}
3141		}
3142	}
3143
3144	ReleaseSysCache(tup);
3145
3146	return visible;
3147}
3148
3149/*
3150 * get_ts_config_oid - find a TS config by possibly qualified name
3151 *
3152 * If not found, returns InvalidOid if missing_ok, else throws error
3153 */
3154Oid
3155get_ts_config_oid(List *names, bool missing_ok)
3156{
3157	char	   *schemaname;
3158	char	   *config_name;
3159	Oid			namespaceId;
3160	Oid			cfgoid = InvalidOid;
3161	ListCell   *l;
3162
3163	/* deconstruct the name list */
3164	DeconstructQualifiedName(names, &schemaname, &config_name);
3165
3166	if (schemaname)
3167	{
3168		/* use exact schema given */
3169		namespaceId = LookupExplicitNamespace(schemaname, missing_ok);
3170		if (missing_ok && !OidIsValid(namespaceId))
3171			cfgoid = InvalidOid;
3172		else
3173			cfgoid = GetSysCacheOid2(TSCONFIGNAMENSP, Anum_pg_ts_config_oid,
3174									 PointerGetDatum(config_name),
3175									 ObjectIdGetDatum(namespaceId));
3176	}
3177	else
3178	{
3179		/* search for it in search path */
3180		recomputeNamespacePath();
3181
3182		foreach(l, activeSearchPath)
3183		{
3184			namespaceId = lfirst_oid(l);
3185
3186			if (namespaceId == myTempNamespace)
3187				continue;		/* do not look in temp namespace */
3188
3189			cfgoid = GetSysCacheOid2(TSCONFIGNAMENSP, Anum_pg_ts_config_oid,
3190									 PointerGetDatum(config_name),
3191									 ObjectIdGetDatum(namespaceId));
3192			if (OidIsValid(cfgoid))
3193				break;
3194		}
3195	}
3196
3197	if (!OidIsValid(cfgoid) && !missing_ok)
3198		ereport(ERROR,
3199				(errcode(ERRCODE_UNDEFINED_OBJECT),
3200				 errmsg("text search configuration \"%s\" does not exist",
3201						NameListToString(names))));
3202
3203	return cfgoid;
3204}
3205
3206/*
3207 * TSConfigIsVisible
3208 *		Determine whether a text search configuration (identified by OID)
3209 *		is visible in the current search path.  Visible means "would be found
3210 *		by searching for the unqualified text search configuration name".
3211 */
3212bool
3213TSConfigIsVisible(Oid cfgid)
3214{
3215	return TSConfigIsVisibleExt(cfgid, NULL);
3216}
3217
3218/*
3219 * TSConfigIsVisibleExt
3220 *		As above, but if the configuration isn't found and is_missing is not
3221 *		NULL, then set *is_missing = true and return false instead of throwing
3222 *		an error.  (Caller must initialize *is_missing = false.)
3223 */
3224static bool
3225TSConfigIsVisibleExt(Oid cfgid, bool *is_missing)
3226{
3227	HeapTuple	tup;
3228	Form_pg_ts_config form;
3229	Oid			namespace;
3230	bool		visible;
3231
3232	tup = SearchSysCache1(TSCONFIGOID, ObjectIdGetDatum(cfgid));
3233	if (!HeapTupleIsValid(tup))
3234	{
3235		if (is_missing != NULL)
3236		{
3237			*is_missing = true;
3238			return false;
3239		}
3240		elog(ERROR, "cache lookup failed for text search configuration %u",
3241			 cfgid);
3242	}
3243	form = (Form_pg_ts_config) GETSTRUCT(tup);
3244
3245	recomputeNamespacePath();
3246
3247	/*
3248	 * Quick check: if it ain't in the path at all, it ain't visible. Items in
3249	 * the system namespace are surely in the path and so we needn't even do
3250	 * list_member_oid() for them.
3251	 */
3252	namespace = form->cfgnamespace;
3253	if (namespace != PG_CATALOG_NAMESPACE &&
3254		!list_member_oid(activeSearchPath, namespace))
3255		visible = false;
3256	else
3257	{
3258		/*
3259		 * If it is in the path, it might still not be visible; it could be
3260		 * hidden by another configuration of the same name earlier in the
3261		 * path. So we must do a slow check for conflicting configurations.
3262		 */
3263		char	   *name = NameStr(form->cfgname);
3264		ListCell   *l;
3265
3266		visible = false;
3267		foreach(l, activeSearchPath)
3268		{
3269			Oid			namespaceId = lfirst_oid(l);
3270
3271			if (namespaceId == myTempNamespace)
3272				continue;		/* do not look in temp namespace */
3273
3274			if (namespaceId == namespace)
3275			{
3276				/* Found it first in path */
3277				visible = true;
3278				break;
3279			}
3280			if (SearchSysCacheExists2(TSCONFIGNAMENSP,
3281									  PointerGetDatum(name),
3282									  ObjectIdGetDatum(namespaceId)))
3283			{
3284				/* Found something else first in path */
3285				break;
3286			}
3287		}
3288	}
3289
3290	ReleaseSysCache(tup);
3291
3292	return visible;
3293}
3294
3295
3296/*
3297 * DeconstructQualifiedName
3298 *		Given a possibly-qualified name expressed as a list of String nodes,
3299 *		extract the schema name and object name.
3300 *
3301 * *nspname_p is set to NULL if there is no explicit schema name.
3302 */
3303void
3304DeconstructQualifiedName(const List *names,
3305						 char **nspname_p,
3306						 char **objname_p)
3307{
3308	char	   *catalogname;
3309	char	   *schemaname = NULL;
3310	char	   *objname = NULL;
3311
3312	switch (list_length(names))
3313	{
3314		case 1:
3315			objname = strVal(linitial(names));
3316			break;
3317		case 2:
3318			schemaname = strVal(linitial(names));
3319			objname = strVal(lsecond(names));
3320			break;
3321		case 3:
3322			catalogname = strVal(linitial(names));
3323			schemaname = strVal(lsecond(names));
3324			objname = strVal(lthird(names));
3325
3326			/*
3327			 * We check the catalog name and then ignore it.
3328			 */
3329			if (strcmp(catalogname, get_database_name(MyDatabaseId)) != 0)
3330				ereport(ERROR,
3331						(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
3332						 errmsg("cross-database references are not implemented: %s",
3333								NameListToString(names))));
3334			break;
3335		default:
3336			ereport(ERROR,
3337					(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
3338					 errmsg("improper qualified name (too many dotted names): %s",
3339							NameListToString(names))));
3340			break;
3341	}
3342
3343	*nspname_p = schemaname;
3344	*objname_p = objname;
3345}
3346
3347/*
3348 * LookupNamespaceNoError
3349 *		Look up a schema name.
3350 *
3351 * Returns the namespace OID, or InvalidOid if not found.
3352 *
3353 * Note this does NOT perform any permissions check --- callers are
3354 * responsible for being sure that an appropriate check is made.
3355 * In the majority of cases LookupExplicitNamespace is preferable.
3356 */
3357Oid
3358LookupNamespaceNoError(const char *nspname)
3359{
3360	/* check for pg_temp alias */
3361	if (strcmp(nspname, "pg_temp") == 0)
3362	{
3363		if (OidIsValid(myTempNamespace))
3364		{
3365			InvokeNamespaceSearchHook(myTempNamespace, true);
3366			return myTempNamespace;
3367		}
3368
3369		/*
3370		 * Since this is used only for looking up existing objects, there is
3371		 * no point in trying to initialize the temp namespace here; and doing
3372		 * so might create problems for some callers. Just report "not found".
3373		 */
3374		return InvalidOid;
3375	}
3376
3377	return get_namespace_oid(nspname, true);
3378}
3379
3380/*
3381 * LookupExplicitNamespace
3382 *		Process an explicitly-specified schema name: look up the schema
3383 *		and verify we have USAGE (lookup) rights in it.
3384 *
3385 * Returns the namespace OID
3386 */
3387Oid
3388LookupExplicitNamespace(const char *nspname, bool missing_ok)
3389{
3390	Oid			namespaceId;
3391	AclResult	aclresult;
3392
3393	/* check for pg_temp alias */
3394	if (strcmp(nspname, "pg_temp") == 0)
3395	{
3396		if (OidIsValid(myTempNamespace))
3397			return myTempNamespace;
3398
3399		/*
3400		 * Since this is used only for looking up existing objects, there is
3401		 * no point in trying to initialize the temp namespace here; and doing
3402		 * so might create problems for some callers --- just fall through.
3403		 */
3404	}
3405
3406	namespaceId = get_namespace_oid(nspname, missing_ok);
3407	if (missing_ok && !OidIsValid(namespaceId))
3408		return InvalidOid;
3409
3410	aclresult = object_aclcheck(NamespaceRelationId, namespaceId, GetUserId(), ACL_USAGE);
3411	if (aclresult != ACLCHECK_OK)
3412		aclcheck_error(aclresult, OBJECT_SCHEMA,
3413					   nspname);
3414	/* Schema search hook for this lookup */
3415	InvokeNamespaceSearchHook(namespaceId, true);
3416
3417	return namespaceId;
3418}
3419
3420/*
3421 * LookupCreationNamespace
3422 *		Look up the schema and verify we have CREATE rights on it.
3423 *
3424 * This is just like LookupExplicitNamespace except for the different
3425 * permission check, and that we are willing to create pg_temp if needed.
3426 *
3427 * Note: calling this may result in a CommandCounterIncrement operation,
3428 * if we have to create or clean out the temp namespace.
3429 */
3430Oid
3431LookupCreationNamespace(const char *nspname)
3432{
3433	Oid			namespaceId;
3434	AclResult	aclresult;
3435
3436	/* check for pg_temp alias */
3437	if (strcmp(nspname, "pg_temp") == 0)
3438	{
3439		/* Initialize temp namespace */
3440		AccessTempTableNamespace(false);
3441		return myTempNamespace;
3442	}
3443
3444	namespaceId = get_namespace_oid(nspname, false);
3445
3446	aclresult = object_aclcheck(NamespaceRelationId, namespaceId, GetUserId(), ACL_CREATE);
3447	if (aclresult != ACLCHECK_OK)
3448		aclcheck_error(aclresult, OBJECT_SCHEMA,
3449					   nspname);
3450
3451	return namespaceId;
3452}
3453
3454/*
3455 * Common checks on switching namespaces.
3456 *
3457 * We complain if either the old or new namespaces is a temporary schema
3458 * (or temporary toast schema), or if either the old or new namespaces is the
3459 * TOAST schema.
3460 */
3461void
3462CheckSetNamespace(Oid oldNspOid, Oid nspOid)
3463{
3464	/* disallow renaming into or out of temp schemas */
3465	if (isAnyTempNamespace(nspOid) || isAnyTempNamespace(oldNspOid))
3466		ereport(ERROR,
3467				(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
3468				 errmsg("cannot move objects into or out of temporary schemas")));
3469
3470	/* same for TOAST schema */
3471	if (nspOid == PG_TOAST_NAMESPACE || oldNspOid == PG_TOAST_NAMESPACE)
3472		ereport(ERROR,
3473				(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
3474				 errmsg("cannot move objects into or out of TOAST schema")));
3475}
3476
3477/*
3478 * QualifiedNameGetCreationNamespace
3479 *		Given a possibly-qualified name for an object (in List-of-Strings
3480 *		format), determine what namespace the object should be created in.
3481 *		Also extract and return the object name (last component of list).
3482 *
3483 * Note: this does not apply any permissions check.  Callers must check
3484 * for CREATE rights on the selected namespace when appropriate.
3485 *
3486 * Note: calling this may result in a CommandCounterIncrement operation,
3487 * if we have to create or clean out the temp namespace.
3488 */
3489Oid
3490QualifiedNameGetCreationNamespace(const List *names, char **objname_p)
3491{
3492	char	   *schemaname;
3493	Oid			namespaceId;
3494
3495	/* deconstruct the name list */
3496	DeconstructQualifiedName(names, &schemaname, objname_p);
3497
3498	if (schemaname)
3499	{
3500		/* check for pg_temp alias */
3501		if (strcmp(schemaname, "pg_temp") == 0)
3502		{
3503			/* Initialize temp namespace */
3504			AccessTempTableNamespace(false);
3505			return myTempNamespace;
3506		}
3507		/* use exact schema given */
3508		namespaceId = get_namespace_oid(schemaname, false);
3509		/* we do not check for USAGE rights here! */
3510	}
3511	else
3512	{
3513		/* use the default creation namespace */
3514		recomputeNamespacePath();
3515		if (activeTempCreationPending)
3516		{
3517			/* Need to initialize temp namespace */
3518			AccessTempTableNamespace(true);
3519			return myTempNamespace;
3520		}
3521		namespaceId = activeCreationNamespace;
3522		if (!OidIsValid(namespaceId))
3523			ereport(ERROR,
3524					(errcode(ERRCODE_UNDEFINED_SCHEMA),
3525					 errmsg("no schema has been selected to create in")));
3526	}
3527
3528	return namespaceId;
3529}
3530
3531/*
3532 * get_namespace_oid - given a namespace name, look up the OID
3533 *
3534 * If missing_ok is false, throw an error if namespace name not found.  If
3535 * true, just return InvalidOid.
3536 */
3537Oid
3538get_namespace_oid(const char *nspname, bool missing_ok)
3539{
3540	Oid			oid;
3541
3542	oid = GetSysCacheOid1(NAMESPACENAME, Anum_pg_namespace_oid,
3543						  CStringGetDatum(nspname));
3544	if (!OidIsValid(oid) && !missing_ok)
3545		ereport(ERROR,
3546				(errcode(ERRCODE_UNDEFINED_SCHEMA),
3547				 errmsg("schema \"%s\" does not exist", nspname)));
3548
3549	return oid;
3550}
3551
3552/*
3553 * makeRangeVarFromNameList
3554 *		Utility routine to convert a qualified-name list into RangeVar form.
3555 */
3556RangeVar *
3557makeRangeVarFromNameList(const List *names)
3558{
3559	RangeVar   *rel = makeRangeVar(NULL, NULL, -1);
3560
3561	switch (list_length(names))
3562	{
3563		case 1:
3564			rel->relname = strVal(linitial(names));
3565			break;
3566		case 2:
3567			rel->schemaname = strVal(linitial(names));
3568			rel->relname = strVal(lsecond(names));
3569			break;
3570		case 3:
3571			rel->catalogname = strVal(linitial(names));
3572			rel->schemaname = strVal(lsecond(names));
3573			rel->relname = strVal(lthird(names));
3574			break;
3575		default:
3576			ereport(ERROR,
3577					(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
3578					 errmsg("improper relation name (too many dotted names): %s",
3579							NameListToString(names))));
3580			break;
3581	}
3582
3583	return rel;
3584}
3585
3586/*
3587 * NameListToString
3588 *		Utility routine to convert a qualified-name list into a string.
3589 *
3590 * This is used primarily to form error messages, and so we do not quote
3591 * the list elements, for the sake of legibility.
3592 *
3593 * In most scenarios the list elements should always be String values,
3594 * but we also allow A_Star for the convenience of ColumnRef processing.
3595 */
3596char *
3597NameListToString(const List *names)
3598{
3599	StringInfoData string;
3600	ListCell   *l;
3601
3602	initStringInfo(&string);
3603
3604	foreach(l, names)
3605	{
3606		Node	   *name = (Node *) lfirst(l);
3607
3608		if (l != list_head(names))
3609			appendStringInfoChar(&string, '.');
3610
3611		if (IsA(name, String))
3612			appendStringInfoString(&string, strVal(name));
3613		else if (IsA(name, A_Star))
3614			appendStringInfoChar(&string, '*');
3615		else
3616			elog(ERROR, "unexpected node type in name list: %d",
3617				 (int) nodeTag(name));
3618	}
3619
3620	return string.data;
3621}
3622
3623/*
3624 * NameListToQuotedString
3625 *		Utility routine to convert a qualified-name list into a string.
3626 *
3627 * Same as above except that names will be double-quoted where necessary,
3628 * so the string could be re-parsed (eg, by textToQualifiedNameList).
3629 */
3630char *
3631NameListToQuotedString(const List *names)
3632{
3633	StringInfoData string;
3634	ListCell   *l;
3635
3636	initStringInfo(&string);
3637
3638	foreach(l, names)
3639	{
3640		if (l != list_head(names))
3641			appendStringInfoChar(&string, '.');
3642		appendStringInfoString(&string, quote_identifier(strVal(lfirst(l))));
3643	}
3644
3645	return string.data;
3646}
3647
3648/*
3649 * isTempNamespace - is the given namespace my temporary-table namespace?
3650 */
3651bool
3652isTempNamespace(Oid namespaceId)
3653{
3654	if (OidIsValid(myTempNamespace) && myTempNamespace == namespaceId)
3655		return true;
3656	return false;
3657}
3658
3659/*
3660 * isTempToastNamespace - is the given namespace my temporary-toast-table
3661 *		namespace?
3662 */
3663bool
3664isTempToastNamespace(Oid namespaceId)
3665{
3666	if (OidIsValid(myTempToastNamespace) && myTempToastNamespace == namespaceId)
3667		return true;
3668	return false;
3669}
3670
3671/*
3672 * isTempOrTempToastNamespace - is the given namespace my temporary-table
3673 *		namespace or my temporary-toast-table namespace?
3674 */
3675bool
3676isTempOrTempToastNamespace(Oid namespaceId)
3677{
3678	if (OidIsValid(myTempNamespace) &&
3679		(myTempNamespace == namespaceId || myTempToastNamespace == namespaceId))
3680		return true;
3681	return false;
3682}
3683
3684/*
3685 * isAnyTempNamespace - is the given namespace a temporary-table namespace
3686 * (either my own, or another backend's)?  Temporary-toast-table namespaces
3687 * are included, too.
3688 */
3689bool
3690isAnyTempNamespace(Oid namespaceId)
3691{
3692	bool		result;
3693	char	   *nspname;
3694
3695	/* True if the namespace name starts with "pg_temp_" or "pg_toast_temp_" */
3696	nspname = get_namespace_name(namespaceId);
3697	if (!nspname)
3698		return false;			/* no such namespace? */
3699	result = (strncmp(nspname, "pg_temp_", 8) == 0) ||
3700		(strncmp(nspname, "pg_toast_temp_", 14) == 0);
3701	pfree(nspname);
3702	return result;
3703}
3704
3705/*
3706 * isOtherTempNamespace - is the given namespace some other backend's
3707 * temporary-table namespace (including temporary-toast-table namespaces)?
3708 *
3709 * Note: for most purposes in the C code, this function is obsolete.  Use
3710 * RELATION_IS_OTHER_TEMP() instead to detect non-local temp relations.
3711 */
3712bool
3713isOtherTempNamespace(Oid namespaceId)
3714{
3715	/* If it's my own temp namespace, say "false" */
3716	if (isTempOrTempToastNamespace(namespaceId))
3717		return false;
3718	/* Else, if it's any temp namespace, say "true" */
3719	return isAnyTempNamespace(namespaceId);
3720}
3721
3722/*
3723 * checkTempNamespaceStatus - is the given namespace owned and actively used
3724 * by a backend?
3725 *
3726 * Note: this can be used while scanning relations in pg_class to detect
3727 * orphaned temporary tables or namespaces with a backend connected to a
3728 * given database.  The result may be out of date quickly, so the caller
3729 * must be careful how to handle this information.
3730 */
3731TempNamespaceStatus
3732checkTempNamespaceStatus(Oid namespaceId)
3733{
3734	PGPROC	   *proc;
3735	ProcNumber	procNumber;
3736
3737	Assert(OidIsValid(MyDatabaseId));
3738
3739	procNumber = GetTempNamespaceProcNumber(namespaceId);
3740
3741	/* No such namespace, or its name shows it's not temp? */
3742	if (procNumber == INVALID_PROC_NUMBER)
3743		return TEMP_NAMESPACE_NOT_TEMP;
3744
3745	/* Is the backend alive? */
3746	proc = ProcNumberGetProc(procNumber);
3747	if (proc == NULL)
3748		return TEMP_NAMESPACE_IDLE;
3749
3750	/* Is the backend connected to the same database we are looking at? */
3751	if (proc->databaseId != MyDatabaseId)
3752		return TEMP_NAMESPACE_IDLE;
3753
3754	/* Does the backend own the temporary namespace? */
3755	if (proc->tempNamespaceId != namespaceId)
3756		return TEMP_NAMESPACE_IDLE;
3757
3758	/* Yup, so namespace is busy */
3759	return TEMP_NAMESPACE_IN_USE;
3760}
3761
3762/*
3763 * GetTempNamespaceProcNumber - if the given namespace is a temporary-table
3764 * namespace (either my own, or another backend's), return the proc number
3765 * that owns it.  Temporary-toast-table namespaces are included, too.
3766 * If it isn't a temp namespace, return INVALID_PROC_NUMBER.
3767 */
3768ProcNumber
3769GetTempNamespaceProcNumber(Oid namespaceId)
3770{
3771	int			result;
3772	char	   *nspname;
3773
3774	/* See if the namespace name starts with "pg_temp_" or "pg_toast_temp_" */
3775	nspname = get_namespace_name(namespaceId);
3776	if (!nspname)
3777		return INVALID_PROC_NUMBER; /* no such namespace? */
3778	if (strncmp(nspname, "pg_temp_", 8) == 0)
3779		result = atoi(nspname + 8);
3780	else if (strncmp(nspname, "pg_toast_temp_", 14) == 0)
3781		result = atoi(nspname + 14);
3782	else
3783		result = INVALID_PROC_NUMBER;
3784	pfree(nspname);
3785	return result;
3786}
3787
3788/*
3789 * GetTempToastNamespace - get the OID of my temporary-toast-table namespace,
3790 * which must already be assigned.  (This is only used when creating a toast
3791 * table for a temp table, so we must have already done InitTempTableNamespace)
3792 */
3793Oid
3794GetTempToastNamespace(void)
3795{
3796	Assert(OidIsValid(myTempToastNamespace));
3797	return myTempToastNamespace;
3798}
3799
3800
3801/*
3802 * GetTempNamespaceState - fetch status of session's temporary namespace
3803 *
3804 * This is used for conveying state to a parallel worker, and is not meant
3805 * for general-purpose access.
3806 */
3807void
3808GetTempNamespaceState(Oid *tempNamespaceId, Oid *tempToastNamespaceId)
3809{
3810	/* Return namespace OIDs, or 0 if session has not created temp namespace */
3811	*tempNamespaceId = myTempNamespace;
3812	*tempToastNamespaceId = myTempToastNamespace;
3813}
3814
3815/*
3816 * SetTempNamespaceState - set status of session's temporary namespace
3817 *
3818 * This is used for conveying state to a parallel worker, and is not meant for
3819 * general-purpose access.  By transferring these namespace OIDs to workers,
3820 * we ensure they will have the same notion of the search path as their leader
3821 * does.
3822 */
3823void
3824SetTempNamespaceState(Oid tempNamespaceId, Oid tempToastNamespaceId)
3825{
3826	/* Worker should not have created its own namespaces ... */
3827	Assert(myTempNamespace == InvalidOid);
3828	Assert(myTempToastNamespace == InvalidOid);
3829	Assert(myTempNamespaceSubID == InvalidSubTransactionId);
3830
3831	/* Assign same namespace OIDs that leader has */
3832	myTempNamespace = tempNamespaceId;
3833	myTempToastNamespace = tempToastNamespaceId;
3834
3835	/*
3836	 * It's fine to leave myTempNamespaceSubID == InvalidSubTransactionId.
3837	 * Even if the namespace is new so far as the leader is concerned, it's
3838	 * not new to the worker, and we certainly wouldn't want the worker trying
3839	 * to destroy it.
3840	 */
3841
3842	baseSearchPathValid = false;	/* may need to rebuild list */
3843	searchPathCacheValid = false;
3844}
3845
3846
3847/*
3848 * GetSearchPathMatcher - fetch current search path definition.
3849 *
3850 * The result structure is allocated in the specified memory context
3851 * (which might or might not be equal to CurrentMemoryContext); but any
3852 * junk created by revalidation calculations will be in CurrentMemoryContext.
3853 */
3854SearchPathMatcher *
3855GetSearchPathMatcher(MemoryContext context)
3856{
3857	SearchPathMatcher *result;
3858	List	   *schemas;
3859	MemoryContext oldcxt;
3860
3861	recomputeNamespacePath();
3862
3863	oldcxt = MemoryContextSwitchTo(context);
3864
3865	result = (SearchPathMatcher *) palloc0(sizeof(SearchPathMatcher));
3866	schemas = list_copy(activeSearchPath);
3867	while (schemas && linitial_oid(schemas) != activeCreationNamespace)
3868	{
3869		if (linitial_oid(schemas) == myTempNamespace)
3870			result->addTemp = true;
3871		else
3872		{
3873			Assert(linitial_oid(schemas) == PG_CATALOG_NAMESPACE);
3874			result->addCatalog = true;
3875		}
3876		schemas = list_delete_first(schemas);
3877	}
3878	result->schemas = schemas;
3879	result->generation = activePathGeneration;
3880
3881	MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcxt);
3882
3883	return result;
3884}
3885
3886/*
3887 * CopySearchPathMatcher - copy the specified SearchPathMatcher.
3888 *
3889 * The result structure is allocated in CurrentMemoryContext.
3890 */
3891SearchPathMatcher *
3892CopySearchPathMatcher(SearchPathMatcher *path)
3893{
3894	SearchPathMatcher *result;
3895
3896	result = (SearchPathMatcher *) palloc(sizeof(SearchPathMatcher));
3897	result->schemas = list_copy(path->schemas);
3898	result->addCatalog = path->addCatalog;
3899	result->addTemp = path->addTemp;
3900	result->generation = path->generation;
3901
3902	return result;
3903}
3904
3905/*
3906 * SearchPathMatchesCurrentEnvironment - does path match current environment?
3907 *
3908 * This is tested over and over in some common code paths, and in the typical
3909 * scenario where the active search path seldom changes, it'll always succeed.
3910 * We make that case fast by keeping a generation counter that is advanced
3911 * whenever the active search path changes.
3912 */
3913bool
3914SearchPathMatchesCurrentEnvironment(SearchPathMatcher *path)
3915{
3916	ListCell   *lc,
3917			   *lcp;
3918
3919	recomputeNamespacePath();
3920
3921	/* Quick out if already known equal to active path. */
3922	if (path->generation == activePathGeneration)
3923		return true;
3924
3925	/* We scan down the activeSearchPath to see if it matches the input. */
3926	lc = list_head(activeSearchPath);
3927
3928	/* If path->addTemp, first item should be my temp namespace. */
3929	if (path->addTemp)
3930	{
3931		if (lc && lfirst_oid(lc) == myTempNamespace)
3932			lc = lnext(activeSearchPath, lc);
3933		else
3934			return false;
3935	}
3936	/* If path->addCatalog, next item should be pg_catalog. */
3937	if (path->addCatalog)
3938	{
3939		if (lc && lfirst_oid(lc) == PG_CATALOG_NAMESPACE)
3940			lc = lnext(activeSearchPath, lc);
3941		else
3942			return false;
3943	}
3944	/* We should now be looking at the activeCreationNamespace. */
3945	if (activeCreationNamespace != (lc ? lfirst_oid(lc) : InvalidOid))
3946		return false;
3947	/* The remainder of activeSearchPath should match path->schemas. */
3948	foreach(lcp, path->schemas)
3949	{
3950		if (lc && lfirst_oid(lc) == lfirst_oid(lcp))
3951			lc = lnext(activeSearchPath, lc);
3952		else
3953			return false;
3954	}
3955	if (lc)
3956		return false;
3957
3958	/*
3959	 * Update path->generation so that future tests will return quickly, so
3960	 * long as the active search path doesn't change.
3961	 */
3962	path->generation = activePathGeneration;
3963
3964	return true;
3965}
3966
3967/*
3968 * get_collation_oid - find a collation by possibly qualified name
3969 *
3970 * Note that this will only find collations that work with the current
3971 * database's encoding.
3972 */
3973Oid
3974get_collation_oid(List *collname, bool missing_ok)
3975{
3976	char	   *schemaname;
3977	char	   *collation_name;
3978	int32		dbencoding = GetDatabaseEncoding();
3979	Oid			namespaceId;
3980	Oid			colloid;
3981	ListCell   *l;
3982
3983	/* deconstruct the name list */
3984	DeconstructQualifiedName(collname, &schemaname, &collation_name);
3985
3986	if (schemaname)
3987	{
3988		/* use exact schema given */
3989		namespaceId = LookupExplicitNamespace(schemaname, missing_ok);
3990		if (missing_ok && !OidIsValid(namespaceId))
3991			return InvalidOid;
3992
3993		colloid = lookup_collation(collation_name, namespaceId, dbencoding);
3994		if (OidIsValid(colloid))
3995			return colloid;
3996	}
3997	else
3998	{
3999		/* search for it in search path */
4000		recomputeNamespacePath();
4001
4002		foreach(l, activeSearchPath)
4003		{
4004			namespaceId = lfirst_oid(l);
4005
4006			if (namespaceId == myTempNamespace)
4007				continue;		/* do not look in temp namespace */
4008
4009			colloid = lookup_collation(collation_name, namespaceId, dbencoding);
4010			if (OidIsValid(colloid))
4011				return colloid;
4012		}
4013	}
4014
4015	/* Not found in path */
4016	if (!missing_ok)
4017		ereport(ERROR,
4018				(errcode(ERRCODE_UNDEFINED_OBJECT),
4019				 errmsg("collation \"%s\" for encoding \"%s\" does not exist",
4020						NameListToString(collname), GetDatabaseEncodingName())));
4021	return InvalidOid;
4022}
4023
4024/*
4025 * get_conversion_oid - find a conversion by possibly qualified name
4026 */
4027Oid
4028get_conversion_oid(List *conname, bool missing_ok)
4029{
4030	char	   *schemaname;
4031	char	   *conversion_name;
4032	Oid			namespaceId;
4033	Oid			conoid = InvalidOid;
4034	ListCell   *l;
4035
4036	/* deconstruct the name list */
4037	DeconstructQualifiedName(conname, &schemaname, &conversion_name);
4038
4039	if (schemaname)
4040	{
4041		/* use exact schema given */
4042		namespaceId = LookupExplicitNamespace(schemaname, missing_ok);
4043		if (missing_ok && !OidIsValid(namespaceId))
4044			conoid = InvalidOid;
4045		else
4046			conoid = GetSysCacheOid2(CONNAMENSP, Anum_pg_conversion_oid,
4047									 PointerGetDatum(conversion_name),
4048									 ObjectIdGetDatum(namespaceId));
4049	}
4050	else
4051	{
4052		/* search for it in search path */
4053		recomputeNamespacePath();
4054
4055		foreach(l, activeSearchPath)
4056		{
4057			namespaceId = lfirst_oid(l);
4058
4059			if (namespaceId == myTempNamespace)
4060				continue;		/* do not look in temp namespace */
4061
4062			conoid = GetSysCacheOid2(CONNAMENSP, Anum_pg_conversion_oid,
4063									 PointerGetDatum(conversion_name),
4064									 ObjectIdGetDatum(namespaceId));
4065			if (OidIsValid(conoid))
4066				return conoid;
4067		}
4068	}
4069
4070	/* Not found in path */
4071	if (!OidIsValid(conoid) && !missing_ok)
4072		ereport(ERROR,
4073				(errcode(ERRCODE_UNDEFINED_OBJECT),
4074				 errmsg("conversion \"%s\" does not exist",
4075						NameListToString(conname))));
4076	return conoid;
4077}
4078
4079/*
4080 * FindDefaultConversionProc - find default encoding conversion proc
4081 */
4082Oid
4083FindDefaultConversionProc(int32 for_encoding, int32 to_encoding)
4084{
4085	Oid			proc;
4086	ListCell   *l;
4087
4088	recomputeNamespacePath();
4089
4090	foreach(l, activeSearchPath)
4091	{
4092		Oid			namespaceId = lfirst_oid(l);
4093
4094		if (namespaceId == myTempNamespace)
4095			continue;			/* do not look in temp namespace */
4096
4097		proc = FindDefaultConversion(namespaceId, for_encoding, to_encoding);
4098		if (OidIsValid(proc))
4099			return proc;
4100	}
4101
4102	/* Not found in path */
4103	return InvalidOid;
4104}
4105
4106/*
4107 * Look up namespace IDs and perform ACL checks. Return newly-allocated list.
4108 */
4109static List *
4110preprocessNamespacePath(const char *searchPath, Oid roleid,
4111						bool *temp_missing)
4112{
4113	char	   *rawname;
4114	List	   *namelist;
4115	List	   *oidlist;
4116	ListCell   *l;
4117
4118	/* Need a modifiable copy */
4119	rawname = pstrdup(searchPath);
4120
4121	/* Parse string into list of identifiers */
4122	if (!SplitIdentifierString(rawname, ',', &namelist))
4123	{
4124		/* syntax error in name list */
4125		/* this should not happen if GUC checked check_search_path */
4126		elog(ERROR, "invalid list syntax");
4127	}
4128
4129	/*
4130	 * Convert the list of names to a list of OIDs.  If any names are not
4131	 * recognizable or we don't have read access, just leave them out of the
4132	 * list.  (We can't raise an error, since the search_path setting has
4133	 * already been accepted.)	Don't make duplicate entries, either.
4134	 */
4135	oidlist = NIL;
4136	*temp_missing = false;
4137	foreach(l, namelist)
4138	{
4139		char	   *curname = (char *) lfirst(l);
4140		Oid			namespaceId;
4141
4142		if (strcmp(curname, "$user") == 0)
4143		{
4144			/* $user --- substitute namespace matching user name, if any */
4145			HeapTuple	tuple;
4146
4147			tuple = SearchSysCache1(AUTHOID, ObjectIdGetDatum(roleid));
4148			if (HeapTupleIsValid(tuple))
4149			{
4150				char	   *rname;
4151
4152				rname = NameStr(((Form_pg_authid) GETSTRUCT(tuple))->rolname);
4153				namespaceId = get_namespace_oid(rname, true);
4154				ReleaseSysCache(tuple);
4155				if (OidIsValid(namespaceId) &&
4156					object_aclcheck(NamespaceRelationId, namespaceId, roleid,
4157									ACL_USAGE) == ACLCHECK_OK)
4158					oidlist = lappend_oid(oidlist, namespaceId);
4159			}
4160		}
4161		else if (strcmp(curname, "pg_temp") == 0)
4162		{
4163			/* pg_temp --- substitute temp namespace, if any */
4164			if (OidIsValid(myTempNamespace))
4165				oidlist = lappend_oid(oidlist, myTempNamespace);
4166			else
4167			{
4168				/* If it ought to be the creation namespace, set flag */
4169				if (oidlist == NIL)
4170					*temp_missing = true;
4171			}
4172		}
4173		else
4174		{
4175			/* normal namespace reference */
4176			namespaceId = get_namespace_oid(curname, true);
4177			if (OidIsValid(namespaceId) &&
4178				object_aclcheck(NamespaceRelationId, namespaceId, roleid,
4179								ACL_USAGE) == ACLCHECK_OK)
4180				oidlist = lappend_oid(oidlist, namespaceId);
4181		}
4182	}
4183
4184	pfree(rawname);
4185	list_free(namelist);
4186
4187	return oidlist;
4188}
4189
4190/*
4191 * Remove duplicates, run namespace search hooks, and prepend
4192 * implicitly-searched namespaces. Return newly-allocated list.
4193 *
4194 * If an object_access_hook is present, this must always be recalculated. It
4195 * may seem that duplicate elimination is not dependent on the result of the
4196 * hook, but if a hook returns different results on different calls for the
4197 * same namespace ID, then it could affect the order in which that namespace
4198 * appears in the final list.
4199 */
4200static List *
4201finalNamespacePath(List *oidlist, Oid *firstNS)
4202{
4203	List	   *finalPath = NIL;
4204	ListCell   *lc;
4205
4206	foreach(lc, oidlist)
4207	{
4208		Oid			namespaceId = lfirst_oid(lc);
4209
4210		if (!list_member_oid(finalPath, namespaceId))
4211		{
4212			if (InvokeNamespaceSearchHook(namespaceId, false))
4213				finalPath = lappend_oid(finalPath, namespaceId);
4214		}
4215	}
4216
4217	/*
4218	 * Remember the first member of the explicit list.  (Note: this is
4219	 * nominally wrong if temp_missing, but we need it anyway to distinguish
4220	 * explicit from implicit mention of pg_catalog.)
4221	 */
4222	if (finalPath == NIL)
4223		*firstNS = InvalidOid;
4224	else
4225		*firstNS = linitial_oid(finalPath);
4226
4227	/*
4228	 * Add any implicitly-searched namespaces to the list.  Note these go on
4229	 * the front, not the back; also notice that we do not check USAGE
4230	 * permissions for these.
4231	 */
4232	if (!list_member_oid(finalPath, PG_CATALOG_NAMESPACE))
4233		finalPath = lcons_oid(PG_CATALOG_NAMESPACE, finalPath);
4234
4235	if (OidIsValid(myTempNamespace) &&
4236		!list_member_oid(finalPath, myTempNamespace))
4237		finalPath = lcons_oid(myTempNamespace, finalPath);
4238
4239	return finalPath;
4240}
4241
4242/*
4243 * Retrieve search path information from the cache; or if not there, fill
4244 * it. The returned entry is valid only until the next call to this function.
4245 */
4246static const SearchPathCacheEntry *
4247cachedNamespacePath(const char *searchPath, Oid roleid)
4248{
4249	MemoryContext oldcxt;
4250	SearchPathCacheEntry *entry;
4251
4252	spcache_init();
4253
4254	entry = spcache_insert(searchPath, roleid);
4255
4256	/*
4257	 * An OOM may have resulted in a cache entry with missing 'oidlist' or
4258	 * 'finalPath', so just compute whatever is missing.
4259	 */
4260
4261	if (entry->oidlist == NIL)
4262	{
4263		oldcxt = MemoryContextSwitchTo(SearchPathCacheContext);
4264		entry->oidlist = preprocessNamespacePath(searchPath, roleid,
4265												 &entry->temp_missing);
4266		MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcxt);
4267	}
4268
4269	/*
4270	 * If a hook is set, we must recompute finalPath from the oidlist each
4271	 * time, because the hook may affect the result. This is still much faster
4272	 * than recomputing from the string (and doing catalog lookups and ACL
4273	 * checks).
4274	 */
4275	if (entry->finalPath == NIL || object_access_hook ||
4276		entry->forceRecompute)
4277	{
4278		list_free(entry->finalPath);
4279		entry->finalPath = NIL;
4280
4281		oldcxt = MemoryContextSwitchTo(SearchPathCacheContext);
4282		entry->finalPath = finalNamespacePath(entry->oidlist,
4283											  &entry->firstNS);
4284		MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcxt);
4285
4286		/*
4287		 * If an object_access_hook is set when finalPath is calculated, the
4288		 * result may be affected by the hook. Force recomputation of
4289		 * finalPath the next time this cache entry is used, even if the
4290		 * object_access_hook is not set at that time.
4291		 */
4292		entry->forceRecompute = object_access_hook ? true : false;
4293	}
4294
4295	return entry;
4296}
4297
4298/*
4299 * recomputeNamespacePath - recompute path derived variables if needed.
4300 */
4301static void
4302recomputeNamespacePath(void)
4303{
4304	Oid			roleid = GetUserId();
4305	bool		pathChanged;
4306	const SearchPathCacheEntry *entry;
4307
4308	/* Do nothing if path is already valid. */
4309	if (baseSearchPathValid && namespaceUser == roleid)
4310		return;
4311
4312	entry = cachedNamespacePath(namespace_search_path, roleid);
4313
4314	if (baseCreationNamespace == entry->firstNS &&
4315		baseTempCreationPending == entry->temp_missing &&
4316		equal(entry->finalPath, baseSearchPath))
4317	{
4318		pathChanged = false;
4319	}
4320	else
4321	{
4322		MemoryContext oldcxt;
4323		List	   *newpath;
4324
4325		pathChanged = true;
4326
4327		/* Must save OID list in permanent storage. */
4328		oldcxt = MemoryContextSwitchTo(TopMemoryContext);
4329		newpath = list_copy(entry->finalPath);
4330		MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcxt);
4331
4332		/* Now safe to assign to state variables. */
4333		list_free(baseSearchPath);
4334		baseSearchPath = newpath;
4335		baseCreationNamespace = entry->firstNS;
4336		baseTempCreationPending = entry->temp_missing;
4337	}
4338
4339	/* Mark the path valid. */
4340	baseSearchPathValid = true;
4341	namespaceUser = roleid;
4342
4343	/* And make it active. */
4344	activeSearchPath = baseSearchPath;
4345	activeCreationNamespace = baseCreationNamespace;
4346	activeTempCreationPending = baseTempCreationPending;
4347
4348	/*
4349	 * Bump the generation only if something actually changed.  (Notice that
4350	 * what we compared to was the old state of the base path variables.)
4351	 */
4352	if (pathChanged)
4353		activePathGeneration++;
4354}
4355
4356/*
4357 * AccessTempTableNamespace
4358 *		Provide access to a temporary namespace, potentially creating it
4359 *		if not present yet.  This routine registers if the namespace gets
4360 *		in use in this transaction.  'force' can be set to true to allow
4361 *		the caller to enforce the creation of the temporary namespace for
4362 *		use in this backend, which happens if its creation is pending.
4363 */
4364static void
4365AccessTempTableNamespace(bool force)
4366{
4367	/*
4368	 * Make note that this temporary namespace has been accessed in this
4369	 * transaction.
4370	 */
4371	MyXactFlags |= XACT_FLAGS_ACCESSEDTEMPNAMESPACE;
4372
4373	/*
4374	 * If the caller attempting to access a temporary schema expects the
4375	 * creation of the namespace to be pending and should be enforced, then go
4376	 * through the creation.
4377	 */
4378	if (!force && OidIsValid(myTempNamespace))
4379		return;
4380
4381	/*
4382	 * The temporary tablespace does not exist yet and is wanted, so
4383	 * initialize it.
4384	 */
4385	InitTempTableNamespace();
4386}
4387
4388/*
4389 * InitTempTableNamespace
4390 *		Initialize temp table namespace on first use in a particular backend
4391 */
4392static void
4393InitTempTableNamespace(void)
4394{
4395	char		namespaceName[NAMEDATALEN];
4396	Oid			namespaceId;
4397	Oid			toastspaceId;
4398
4399	Assert(!OidIsValid(myTempNamespace));
4400
4401	/*
4402	 * First, do permission check to see if we are authorized to make temp
4403	 * tables.  We use a nonstandard error message here since "databasename:
4404	 * permission denied" might be a tad cryptic.
4405	 *
4406	 * Note that ACL_CREATE_TEMP rights are rechecked in pg_namespace_aclmask;
4407	 * that's necessary since current user ID could change during the session.
4408	 * But there's no need to make the namespace in the first place until a
4409	 * temp table creation request is made by someone with appropriate rights.
4410	 */
4411	if (object_aclcheck(DatabaseRelationId, MyDatabaseId, GetUserId(),
4412						ACL_CREATE_TEMP) != ACLCHECK_OK)
4413		ereport(ERROR,
4414				(errcode(ERRCODE_INSUFFICIENT_PRIVILEGE),
4415				 errmsg("permission denied to create temporary tables in database \"%s\"",
4416						get_database_name(MyDatabaseId))));
4417
4418	/*
4419	 * Do not allow a Hot Standby session to make temp tables.  Aside from
4420	 * problems with modifying the system catalogs, there is a naming
4421	 * conflict: pg_temp_N belongs to the session with proc number N on the
4422	 * primary, not to a hot standby session with the same proc number.  We
4423	 * should not be able to get here anyway due to XactReadOnly checks, but
4424	 * let's just make real sure.  Note that this also backstops various
4425	 * operations that allow XactReadOnly transactions to modify temp tables;
4426	 * they'd need RecoveryInProgress checks if not for this.
4427	 */
4428	if (RecoveryInProgress())
4429		ereport(ERROR,
4430				(errcode(ERRCODE_READ_ONLY_SQL_TRANSACTION),
4431				 errmsg("cannot create temporary tables during recovery")));
4432
4433	/* Parallel workers can't create temporary tables, either. */
4434	if (IsParallelWorker())
4435		ereport(ERROR,
4436				(errcode(ERRCODE_READ_ONLY_SQL_TRANSACTION),
4437				 errmsg("cannot create temporary tables during a parallel operation")));
4438
4439	snprintf(namespaceName, sizeof(namespaceName), "pg_temp_%d", MyProcNumber);
4440
4441	namespaceId = get_namespace_oid(namespaceName, true);
4442	if (!OidIsValid(namespaceId))
4443	{
4444		/*
4445		 * First use of this temp namespace in this database; create it. The
4446		 * temp namespaces are always owned by the superuser.  We leave their
4447		 * permissions at default --- i.e., no access except to superuser ---
4448		 * to ensure that unprivileged users can't peek at other backends'
4449		 * temp tables.  This works because the places that access the temp
4450		 * namespace for my own backend skip permissions checks on it.
4451		 */
4452		namespaceId = NamespaceCreate(namespaceName, BOOTSTRAP_SUPERUSERID,
4453									  true);
4454		/* Advance command counter to make namespace visible */
4455		CommandCounterIncrement();
4456	}
4457	else
4458	{
4459		/*
4460		 * If the namespace already exists, clean it out (in case the former
4461		 * owner crashed without doing so).
4462		 */
4463		RemoveTempRelations(namespaceId);
4464	}
4465
4466	/*
4467	 * If the corresponding toast-table namespace doesn't exist yet, create
4468	 * it. (We assume there is no need to clean it out if it does exist, since
4469	 * dropping a parent table should make its toast table go away.)
4470	 */
4471	snprintf(namespaceName, sizeof(namespaceName), "pg_toast_temp_%d",
4472			 MyProcNumber);
4473
4474	toastspaceId = get_namespace_oid(namespaceName, true);
4475	if (!OidIsValid(toastspaceId))
4476	{
4477		toastspaceId = NamespaceCreate(namespaceName, BOOTSTRAP_SUPERUSERID,
4478									   true);
4479		/* Advance command counter to make namespace visible */
4480		CommandCounterIncrement();
4481	}
4482
4483	/*
4484	 * Okay, we've prepared the temp namespace ... but it's not committed yet,
4485	 * so all our work could be undone by transaction rollback.  Set flag for
4486	 * AtEOXact_Namespace to know what to do.
4487	 */
4488	myTempNamespace = namespaceId;
4489	myTempToastNamespace = toastspaceId;
4490
4491	/*
4492	 * Mark MyProc as owning this namespace which other processes can use to
4493	 * decide if a temporary namespace is in use or not.  We assume that
4494	 * assignment of namespaceId is an atomic operation.  Even if it is not,
4495	 * the temporary relation which resulted in the creation of this temporary
4496	 * namespace is still locked until the current transaction commits, and
4497	 * its pg_namespace row is not visible yet.  However it does not matter:
4498	 * this flag makes the namespace as being in use, so no objects created on
4499	 * it would be removed concurrently.
4500	 */
4501	MyProc->tempNamespaceId = namespaceId;
4502
4503	/* It should not be done already. */
4504	Assert(myTempNamespaceSubID == InvalidSubTransactionId);
4505	myTempNamespaceSubID = GetCurrentSubTransactionId();
4506
4507	baseSearchPathValid = false;	/* need to rebuild list */
4508	searchPathCacheValid = false;
4509}
4510
4511/*
4512 * End-of-transaction cleanup for namespaces.
4513 */
4514void
4515AtEOXact_Namespace(bool isCommit, bool parallel)
4516{
4517	/*
4518	 * If we abort the transaction in which a temp namespace was selected,
4519	 * we'll have to do any creation or cleanout work over again.  So, just
4520	 * forget the namespace entirely until next time.  On the other hand, if
4521	 * we commit then register an exit callback to clean out the temp tables
4522	 * at backend shutdown.  (We only want to register the callback once per
4523	 * session, so this is a good place to do it.)
4524	 */
4525	if (myTempNamespaceSubID != InvalidSubTransactionId && !parallel)
4526	{
4527		if (isCommit)
4528			before_shmem_exit(RemoveTempRelationsCallback, 0);
4529		else
4530		{
4531			myTempNamespace = InvalidOid;
4532			myTempToastNamespace = InvalidOid;
4533			baseSearchPathValid = false;	/* need to rebuild list */
4534			searchPathCacheValid = false;
4535
4536			/*
4537			 * Reset the temporary namespace flag in MyProc.  We assume that
4538			 * this operation is atomic.
4539			 *
4540			 * Because this transaction is aborting, the pg_namespace row is
4541			 * not visible to anyone else anyway, but that doesn't matter:
4542			 * it's not a problem if objects contained in this namespace are
4543			 * removed concurrently.
4544			 */
4545			MyProc->tempNamespaceId = InvalidOid;
4546		}
4547		myTempNamespaceSubID = InvalidSubTransactionId;
4548	}
4549
4550}
4551
4552/*
4553 * AtEOSubXact_Namespace
4554 *
4555 * At subtransaction commit, propagate the temp-namespace-creation
4556 * flag to the parent subtransaction.
4557 *
4558 * At subtransaction abort, forget the flag if we set it up.
4559 */
4560void
4561AtEOSubXact_Namespace(bool isCommit, SubTransactionId mySubid,
4562					  SubTransactionId parentSubid)
4563{
4564
4565	if (myTempNamespaceSubID == mySubid)
4566	{
4567		if (isCommit)
4568			myTempNamespaceSubID = parentSubid;
4569		else
4570		{
4571			myTempNamespaceSubID = InvalidSubTransactionId;
4572			/* TEMP namespace creation failed, so reset state */
4573			myTempNamespace = InvalidOid;
4574			myTempToastNamespace = InvalidOid;
4575			baseSearchPathValid = false;	/* need to rebuild list */
4576			searchPathCacheValid = false;
4577
4578			/*
4579			 * Reset the temporary namespace flag in MyProc.  We assume that
4580			 * this operation is atomic.
4581			 *
4582			 * Because this subtransaction is aborting, the pg_namespace row
4583			 * is not visible to anyone else anyway, but that doesn't matter:
4584			 * it's not a problem if objects contained in this namespace are
4585			 * removed concurrently.
4586			 */
4587			MyProc->tempNamespaceId = InvalidOid;
4588		}
4589	}
4590}
4591
4592/*
4593 * Remove all relations in the specified temp namespace.
4594 *
4595 * This is called at backend shutdown (if we made any temp relations).
4596 * It is also called when we begin using a pre-existing temp namespace,
4597 * in order to clean out any relations that might have been created by
4598 * a crashed backend.
4599 */
4600static void
4601RemoveTempRelations(Oid tempNamespaceId)
4602{
4603	ObjectAddress object;
4604
4605	/*
4606	 * We want to get rid of everything in the target namespace, but not the
4607	 * namespace itself (deleting it only to recreate it later would be a
4608	 * waste of cycles).  Hence, specify SKIP_ORIGINAL.  It's also an INTERNAL
4609	 * deletion, and we want to not drop any extensions that might happen to
4610	 * own temp objects.
4611	 */
4612	object.classId = NamespaceRelationId;
4613	object.objectId = tempNamespaceId;
4614	object.objectSubId = 0;
4615
4616	performDeletion(&object, DROP_CASCADE,
4617					PERFORM_DELETION_INTERNAL |
4618					PERFORM_DELETION_QUIETLY |
4619					PERFORM_DELETION_SKIP_ORIGINAL |
4620					PERFORM_DELETION_SKIP_EXTENSIONS);
4621}
4622
4623/*
4624 * Callback to remove temp relations at backend exit.
4625 */
4626static void
4627RemoveTempRelationsCallback(int code, Datum arg)
4628{
4629	if (OidIsValid(myTempNamespace))	/* should always be true */
4630	{
4631		/* Need to ensure we have a usable transaction. */
4632		AbortOutOfAnyTransaction();
4633		StartTransactionCommand();
4634		PushActiveSnapshot(GetTransactionSnapshot());
4635
4636		RemoveTempRelations(myTempNamespace);
4637
4638		PopActiveSnapshot();
4639		CommitTransactionCommand();
4640	}
4641}
4642
4643/*
4644 * Remove all temp tables from the temporary namespace.
4645 */
4646void
4647ResetTempTableNamespace(void)
4648{
4649	if (OidIsValid(myTempNamespace))
4650		RemoveTempRelations(myTempNamespace);
4651}
4652
4653
4654/*
4655 * Routines for handling the GUC variable 'search_path'.
4656 */
4657
4658/* check_hook: validate new search_path value */
4659bool
4660check_search_path(char **newval, void **extra, GucSource source)
4661{
4662	Oid			roleid = InvalidOid;
4663	const char *searchPath = *newval;
4664	char	   *rawname;
4665	List	   *namelist;
4666	bool		use_cache = (SearchPathCacheContext != NULL);
4667
4668	/*
4669	 * We used to try to check that the named schemas exist, but there are
4670	 * many valid use-cases for having search_path settings that include
4671	 * schemas that don't exist; and often, we are not inside a transaction
4672	 * here and so can't consult the system catalogs anyway.  So now, the only
4673	 * requirement is syntactic validity of the identifier list.
4674	 *
4675	 * Checking only the syntactic validity also allows us to use the search
4676	 * path cache (if available) to avoid calling SplitIdentifierString() on
4677	 * the same string repeatedly.
4678	 */
4679	if (use_cache)
4680	{
4681		spcache_init();
4682
4683		roleid = GetUserId();
4684
4685		if (spcache_lookup(searchPath, roleid) != NULL)
4686			return true;
4687	}
4688
4689	/*
4690	 * Ensure validity check succeeds before creating cache entry.
4691	 */
4692
4693	rawname = pstrdup(searchPath);	/* need a modifiable copy */
4694
4695	/* Parse string into list of identifiers */
4696	if (!SplitIdentifierString(rawname, ',', &namelist))
4697	{
4698		/* syntax error in name list */
4699		GUC_check_errdetail("List syntax is invalid.");
4700		pfree(rawname);
4701		list_free(namelist);
4702		return false;
4703	}
4704	pfree(rawname);
4705	list_free(namelist);
4706
4707	/* OK to create empty cache entry */
4708	if (use_cache)
4709		(void) spcache_insert(searchPath, roleid);
4710
4711	return true;
4712}
4713
4714/* assign_hook: do extra actions as needed */
4715void
4716assign_search_path(const char *newval, void *extra)
4717{
4718	/* don't access search_path during bootstrap */
4719	Assert(!IsBootstrapProcessingMode());
4720
4721	/*
4722	 * We mark the path as needing recomputation, but don't do anything until
4723	 * it's needed.  This avoids trying to do database access during GUC
4724	 * initialization, or outside a transaction.
4725	 *
4726	 * This does not invalidate the search path cache, so if this value had
4727	 * been previously set and no syscache invalidations happened,
4728	 * recomputation may not be necessary.
4729	 */
4730	baseSearchPathValid = false;
4731}
4732
4733/*
4734 * InitializeSearchPath: initialize module during InitPostgres.
4735 *
4736 * This is called after we are up enough to be able to do catalog lookups.
4737 */
4738void
4739InitializeSearchPath(void)
4740{
4741	if (IsBootstrapProcessingMode())
4742	{
4743		/*
4744		 * In bootstrap mode, the search path must be 'pg_catalog' so that
4745		 * tables are created in the proper namespace; ignore the GUC setting.
4746		 */
4747		MemoryContext oldcxt;
4748
4749		oldcxt = MemoryContextSwitchTo(TopMemoryContext);
4750		baseSearchPath = list_make1_oid(PG_CATALOG_NAMESPACE);
4751		MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcxt);
4752		baseCreationNamespace = PG_CATALOG_NAMESPACE;
4753		baseTempCreationPending = false;
4754		baseSearchPathValid = true;
4755		namespaceUser = GetUserId();
4756		activeSearchPath = baseSearchPath;
4757		activeCreationNamespace = baseCreationNamespace;
4758		activeTempCreationPending = baseTempCreationPending;
4759		activePathGeneration++; /* pro forma */
4760	}
4761	else
4762	{
4763		/*
4764		 * In normal mode, arrange for a callback on any syscache invalidation
4765		 * that will affect the search_path cache.
4766		 */
4767
4768		/* namespace name or ACLs may have changed */
4769		CacheRegisterSyscacheCallback(NAMESPACEOID,
4770									  InvalidationCallback,
4771									  (Datum) 0);
4772
4773		/* role name may affect the meaning of "$user" */
4774		CacheRegisterSyscacheCallback(AUTHOID,
4775									  InvalidationCallback,
4776									  (Datum) 0);
4777
4778		/* role membership may affect ACLs */
4779		CacheRegisterSyscacheCallback(AUTHMEMROLEMEM,
4780									  InvalidationCallback,
4781									  (Datum) 0);
4782
4783		/* database owner may affect ACLs */
4784		CacheRegisterSyscacheCallback(DATABASEOID,
4785									  InvalidationCallback,
4786									  (Datum) 0);
4787
4788		/* Force search path to be recomputed on next use */
4789		baseSearchPathValid = false;
4790		searchPathCacheValid = false;
4791	}
4792}
4793
4794/*
4795 * InvalidationCallback
4796 *		Syscache inval callback function
4797 */
4798static void
4799InvalidationCallback(Datum arg, int cacheid, uint32 hashvalue)
4800{
4801	/*
4802	 * Force search path to be recomputed on next use, also invalidating the
4803	 * search path cache (because namespace names, ACLs, or role names may
4804	 * have changed).
4805	 */
4806	baseSearchPathValid = false;
4807	searchPathCacheValid = false;
4808}
4809
4810/*
4811 * Fetch the active search path. The return value is a palloc'ed list
4812 * of OIDs; the caller is responsible for freeing this storage as
4813 * appropriate.
4814 *
4815 * The returned list includes the implicitly-prepended namespaces only if
4816 * includeImplicit is true.
4817 *
4818 * Note: calling this may result in a CommandCounterIncrement operation,
4819 * if we have to create or clean out the temp namespace.
4820 */
4821List *
4822fetch_search_path(bool includeImplicit)
4823{
4824	List	   *result;
4825
4826	recomputeNamespacePath();
4827
4828	/*
4829	 * If the temp namespace should be first, force it to exist.  This is so
4830	 * that callers can trust the result to reflect the actual default
4831	 * creation namespace.  It's a bit bogus to do this here, since
4832	 * current_schema() is supposedly a stable function without side-effects,
4833	 * but the alternatives seem worse.
4834	 */
4835	if (activeTempCreationPending)
4836	{
4837		AccessTempTableNamespace(true);
4838		recomputeNamespacePath();
4839	}
4840
4841	result = list_copy(activeSearchPath);
4842	if (!includeImplicit)
4843	{
4844		while (result && linitial_oid(result) != activeCreationNamespace)
4845			result = list_delete_first(result);
4846	}
4847
4848	return result;
4849}
4850
4851/*
4852 * Fetch the active search path into a caller-allocated array of OIDs.
4853 * Returns the number of path entries.  (If this is more than sarray_len,
4854 * then the data didn't fit and is not all stored.)
4855 *
4856 * The returned list always includes the implicitly-prepended namespaces,
4857 * but never includes the temp namespace.  (This is suitable for existing
4858 * users, which would want to ignore the temp namespace anyway.)  This
4859 * definition allows us to not worry about initializing the temp namespace.
4860 */
4861int
4862fetch_search_path_array(Oid *sarray, int sarray_len)
4863{
4864	int			count = 0;
4865	ListCell   *l;
4866
4867	recomputeNamespacePath();
4868
4869	foreach(l, activeSearchPath)
4870	{
4871		Oid			namespaceId = lfirst_oid(l);
4872
4873		if (namespaceId == myTempNamespace)
4874			continue;			/* do not include temp namespace */
4875
4876		if (count < sarray_len)
4877			sarray[count] = namespaceId;
4878		count++;
4879	}
4880
4881	return count;
4882}
4883
4884
4885/*
4886 * Export the FooIsVisible functions as SQL-callable functions.
4887 *
4888 * Note: as of Postgres 8.4, these will silently return NULL if called on
4889 * a nonexistent object OID, rather than failing.  This is to avoid race
4890 * condition errors when a query that's scanning a catalog using an MVCC
4891 * snapshot uses one of these functions.  The underlying IsVisible functions
4892 * always use an up-to-date snapshot and so might see the object as already
4893 * gone when it's still visible to the transaction snapshot.
4894 */
4895
4896Datum
4897pg_table_is_visible(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
4898{
4899	Oid			oid = PG_GETARG_OID(0);
4900	bool		result;
4901	bool		is_missing = false;
4902
4903	result = RelationIsVisibleExt(oid, &is_missing);
4904
4905	if (is_missing)
4906		PG_RETURN_NULL();
4907	PG_RETURN_BOOL(result);
4908}
4909
4910Datum
4911pg_type_is_visible(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
4912{
4913	Oid			oid = PG_GETARG_OID(0);
4914	bool		result;
4915	bool		is_missing = false;
4916
4917	result = TypeIsVisibleExt(oid, &is_missing);
4918
4919	if (is_missing)
4920		PG_RETURN_NULL();
4921	PG_RETURN_BOOL(result);
4922}
4923
4924Datum
4925pg_function_is_visible(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
4926{
4927	Oid			oid = PG_GETARG_OID(0);
4928	bool		result;
4929	bool		is_missing = false;
4930
4931	result = FunctionIsVisibleExt(oid, &is_missing);
4932
4933	if (is_missing)
4934		PG_RETURN_NULL();

4936}

5077
5078Datum
5079pg_my_temp_schema(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
5080{
5081	PG_RETURN_OID(myTempNamespace);
5082}
5083
5084Datum
5085pg_is_other_temp_schema(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
5086{
5087	Oid			oid = PG_GETARG_OID(0);
5088
5089	PG_RETURN_BOOL(isOtherTempNamespace(oid));
5090}
